Test 36: Maximum Independent Set (C++) Score: 0.00 / 13
Typical Prompt:
You are writing C++ code to solve the **Maximum Independent Set** problem.

Given an undirected graph, find the largest set of vertices such that no two chosen vertices share an edge.

The evaluator is strict: only an exact maximum independent set is accepted.

**Input format (stdin)**
```
n m
u v
u v
... (m lines total)
```
- `n` = number of vertices (0-indexed: 0..n-1)
- `m` = number of undirected edges
- each edge appears as two integers `u v`

**Output format (stdout)**
```
k
v1 v2 v3 ... vk
```
- `k` = size of your independent set
- second line lists exactly `k` distinct vertex indices (space-separated)
- vertices may be printed in any order

**What will be checked**
1. Output format is valid.
2. Every printed vertex is in range.
3. No edge exists between any two printed vertices.
4. `k` is the true maximum for the input graph.

**Requirements**
1. Use standard C++ (C++17-compatible).
2. Read from stdin and write to stdout.
3. Handle small and very large graphs.
4. Finish within the test timeout.

**Environment**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3

View chain of thought

Find the largest group of nodes in a graph such that no two nodes in the group are connected by an edge. Think of seating people at a dinner party where certain pairs don’t get along — seat as many guests as possible with no feuding neighbours at the same table.

This is NP-hard. The test uses planted instances with a known optimal answer for strict pass/fail grading. Subpasses increase the graph size.

 0.0000
Compilation error: Compilation failed:
tmplkiamo6y.cpp
C:\Users\Ash\AppData\Local\Temp\tmplkiamo6y.cpp(1): fatal error C1083: Cannot open include file: 'bits/stdc++.h': No such file or directory

Maximum Independent Set - tiny (60 vertices), planted MIS=20

Graph: 60 vertices, ~200 edges, planted optimum size = 20

View C++ Code (2531 chars)
#include <bits/stdc++.h>
using namespace std;

int n, m;
vector<vector<int>> adj;
vector<int> best_set;

// Greedy upper bound: compute maximum greedy independent set on remaining vertices
int greedy_ub(const vector<bool>& removed) {
    vector<bool> used(n, false);
    int count = 0;
    for (int u = 0; u < n; u++) {
        if (!removed[u] && !used[u]) {
            count++;
            used[u] = true;
            for (int v : adj[u]) {
                used[v] = true;
            }
        }
    }
    return count;
}

void solve(vector<int>& current, vector<bool>& removed) {
    // Pruning: if current size + upper bound <= best found, return
    int potential = (int)current.size() + greedy_ub(removed);
    if (potential <= (int)best_set.size()) return;

    // Find next vertex with minimum degree for better branching
    int next = -1;
    int min_degree = n + 1;
    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
        if (!removed[i]) {
            int degree = 0;
            for (int j : adj[i]) {
                if (!removed[j]) degree++;
            }
            if (degree < min_degree) {
                min_degree = degree;
                next = i;
            }
        }
    }

    // Base case: all vertices considered
    if (next == -1) {
        if (current.size() > best_set.size()) {
            best_set = current;
        }
        return;
    }

    // Branch 1: include next vertex (remove it and all neighbors)
    current.push_back(next);
    vector<bool> new_removed = removed;
    new_removed[next] = true;
    for (int neighbor : adj[next]) {
        new_removed[neighbor] = true;
    }
    solve(current, new_removed);
    current.pop_back();

    // Branch 2: exclude next vertex
    removed[next] = true;
    solve(current, removed);
    removed[next] = false;
}

int main() {
    ios_base::sync_with_stdio(false);
    cin.tie(nullptr);

    cin >> n >> m;
    adj.assign(n, vector<int>());

    for (int i = 0; i < m; i++) {
        int u, v;
        cin >> u >> v;
        adj[u].push_back(v);
        adj[v].push_back(u);
    }

    // Special case: empty graph (no edges)
    if (m == 0) {
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            best_set.push_back(i);
        }
    } else {
        vector<int> current;
        vector<bool> removed(n, false);
        solve(current, removed);
    }

    // Output
    cout << best_set.size() << "\n";
    for (int i = 0; i < best_set.size(); i++) {
        if (i > 0) cout << " ";
        cout << best_set[i];
    }
    cout << "\n";

    return 0;
}

#include <bits/stdc++.h>
using namespace std;

int n, m;
vector<vector<int>> adj;
vector<int> best_set;

// Greedy upper bound: compute maximum greedy independent set on remaining vertices
int greedy_ub(const vector<bool>& removed) {
    vector<bool> used(n, false);
    int count = 0;
    for (int u = 0; u < n; u++) {
        if (!removed[u] && !used[u]) {
            count++;
            used[u] = true;
            for (int v : adj[u]) {
                used[v] = true;
            }
        }
    }
    return count;
}

void solve(vector<int>& current, vector<bool>& removed) {
    // Pruning: if current size + upper bound <= best found, return
    int potential = (int)current.size() + greedy_ub(removed);
    if (potential <= (int)best_set.size()) return;

    // Find next vertex with minimum degree for better branching
    int next = -1;
    int min_degree = n + 1;
    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
        if (!removed[i]) {
            int degree = 0;
            for (int j : adj[i]) {
                if (!removed[j]) degree++;
            }
            if (degree < min_degree) {
                min_degree = degree;
                next = i;
            }
        }
    }

    // Base case: all vertices considered
    if (next == -1) {
        if (current.size() > best_set.size()) {
            best_set = current;
        }
        return;
    }

    // Branch 1: include next vertex (remove it and all neighbors)
    current.push_back(next);
    vector<bool> new_removed = removed;
    new_removed[next] = true;
    for (int neighbor : adj[next]) {
        new_removed[neighbor] = true;
    }
    solve(current, new_removed);
    current.pop_back();

    // Branch 2: exclude next vertex
    removed[next] = true;
    solve(current, removed);
    removed[next] = false;
}

int main() {
    ios_base::sync_with_stdio(false);
    cin.tie(nullptr);

    cin >> n >> m;
    adj.assign(n, vector<int>());

    for (int i = 0; i < m; i++) {
        int u, v;
        cin >> u >> v;
        adj[u].push_back(v);
        adj[v].push_back(u);
    }

    // Special case: empty graph (no edges)
    if (m == 0) {
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            best_set.push_back(i);
        }
    } else {
        vector<int> current;
        vector<bool> removed(n, false);
        solve(current, removed);
    }

    // Output
    cout << best_set.size() << "\n";
    for (int i = 0; i < best_set.size(); i++) {
        if (i > 0) cout << " ";
        cout << best_set[i];
    }
    cout << "\n";

    return 0;
}

Test 37: Feedback Vertex Set (C#) Score: 0.00 / 14
Typical Prompt:
You are writing C# code to solve the **Directed Feedback Vertex Set** problem.

Given a directed graph, find the smallest set of vertices to remove so the remaining graph has **no directed cycles** (i.e., it becomes a DAG).

The evaluator is strict: only a minimum-size valid feedback vertex set is accepted.

**Input format (stdin)**
```
n m
u v
u v
... (m lines total)
```
- `n`: number of vertices (0-indexed: `0..n-1`)
- `m`: number of directed edges
- each line `u v` means an edge `u -> v`

**Output format (stdout)**
```
k
v1 v2 v3 ... vk
```
- `k`: number of removed vertices
- second line: exactly `k` distinct vertex indices (any order)

**What is checked**
1. Output format is valid.
2. Vertex indices are valid and distinct.
3. Removing those vertices makes the graph acyclic.
4. The set size is minimum.

**Requirements**
1. Provide complete C# code with `Main`.
2. Read from stdin and write to stdout.
3. Handle small and very large graphs.
4. Finish within the test timeout.

**Environment**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3

View chain of thought

Find the smallest set of nodes to remove from a directed graph so that no cycles remain. Think of breaking deadlocks in a dependency chain — remove as few tasks as possible so that the rest can be completed in a straight order.

This is NP-hard. The test uses planted instances where a known small set of nodes creates all the cycles, enabling strict pass/fail grading. Subpasses increase the graph size.

Feedback Vertex Set - tiny: DAG=40, breakers=4

View C# Code (5942 chars)
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Linq;

class Program
{
    static void Main()
    {
        string[] line = Console.ReadLine().Split();
        int n = int.Parse(line[0]);
        int m = int.Parse(line[1]);

        List<int>[] adj = new List<int>[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
            adj[i] = new List<int>();

        for (int i = 0; i < m; i++)
        {
            string[] edge = Console.ReadLine().Split();
            int u = int.Parse(edge[0]);
            int v = int.Parse(edge[1]);
            adj[u].Add(v);
        }

        List<int> fvs = SolveDFVS(n, adj);

        Console.WriteLine(fvs.Count);
        if (fvs.Count > 0)
            Console.WriteLine(string.Join(" ", fvs));
        else
            Console.WriteLine();
    }

    static List<int> SolveDFVS(int n, List<int>[] adj)
    {
        List<HashSet<int>> sccs = FindSCCs(n, adj);

        HashSet<int> result = new HashSet<int>();

        foreach (var scc in sccs)
        {
            if (scc.Count > 1)
            {
                List<int> fvsForScc = FindMinimalFVSForSCC(scc.ToList(), adj);
                foreach (var v in fvsForScc)
                    result.Add(v);
            }
        }

        return result.OrderBy(x => x).ToList();
    }

    static List<HashSet<int>> FindSCCs(int n, List<int>[] adj)
    {
        bool[] visited = new bool[n];
        Stack<int> finish = new Stack<int>();

        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
        {
            if (!visited[i])
                DFS1(i, adj, visited, finish);
        }

        List<int>[] radj = new List<int>[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
            radj[i] = new List<int>();

        for (int u = 0; u < n; u++)
        {
            foreach (var v in adj[u])
                radj[v].Add(u);
        }

        visited = new bool[n];
        List<HashSet<int>> sccs = new List<HashSet<int>>();

        while (finish.Count > 0)
        {
            int u = finish.Pop();
            if (!visited[u])
            {
                HashSet<int> scc = new HashSet<int>();
                DFS2(u, radj, visited, scc);
                sccs.Add(scc);
            }
        }

        return sccs;
    }

    static void DFS1(int u, List<int>[] adj, bool[] visited, Stack<int> finish)
    {
        visited[u] = true;
        foreach (var v in adj[u])
        {
            if (!visited[v])
                DFS1(v, adj, visited, finish);
        }
        finish.Push(u);
    }

    static void DFS2(int u, List<int>[] radj, bool[] visited, HashSet<int> scc)
    {
        visited[u] = true;
        scc.Add(u);
        foreach (var v in radj[u])
        {
            if (!visited[v])
                DFS2(v, radj, visited, scc);
        }
    }

    static List<int> FindMinimalFVSForSCC(List<int> scc, List<int>[] adj)
    {
        foreach (var v in scc)
        {
            if (IsAcyclicAfterRemoval(scc, new List<int> { v }, adj))
                return new List<int> { v };
        }

        if (scc.Count <= 15)
        {
            for (int i = 0; i < scc.Count; i++)
            {
                for (int j = i + 1; j < scc.Count; j++)
                {
                    var removal = new List<int> { scc[i], scc[j] };
                    if (IsAcyclicAfterRemoval(scc, removal, adj))
                        return removal;
                }
            }
        }

        return GreedyRemoval(scc, adj);
    }

    static bool IsAcyclicAfterRemoval(List<int> scc, List<int> removed, List<int>[] adj)
    {
        HashSet<int> removedSet = new HashSet<int>(removed);
        HashSet<int> sccSet = new HashSet<int>(scc);

        HashSet<int> visited = new HashSet<int>();
        HashSet<int> recStack = new HashSet<int>();

        foreach (var u in scc)
        {
            if (!removedSet.Contains(u) && !visited.Contains(u))
            {
                if (HasCycleDFS(u, adj, sccSet, removedSet, visited, recStack))
                    return false;
            }
        }

        return true;
    }

    static bool HasCycleDFS(int u, List<int>[] adj, HashSet<int> sccSet, HashSet<int> removed, HashSet<int> visited, HashSet<int> recStack)
    {
        visited.Add(u);
        recStack.Add(u);

        foreach (var v in adj[u])
        {
            if (!sccSet.Contains(v) || removed.Contains(v))
                continue;

            if (!visited.Contains(v))
            {
                if (HasCycleDFS(v, adj, sccSet, removed, visited, recStack))
                    return true;
            }
            else if (recStack.Contains(v))
            {
                return true;
            }
        }

        recStack.Remove(u);
        return false;
    }

    static List<int> GreedyRemoval(List<int> scc, List<int>[] adj)
    {
        List<int> result = new List<int>();
        HashSet<int> remaining = new HashSet<int>(scc);

        while (remaining.Count > 0)
        {
            if (IsAcyclicAfterRemoval(remaining.ToList(), new List<int>(), adj))
                break;

            int maxDegree = -1;
            int maxVertex = -1;

            foreach (var u in remaining)
            {
                int degree = 0;
                foreach (var v in adj[u])
                {
                    if (remaining.Contains(v))
                        degree++;
                }
                foreach (var v in remaining)
                {
                    if (v != u && adj[v].Contains(u))
                        degree++;
                }

                if (degree > maxDegree)
                {
                    maxDegree = degree;
                    maxVertex = u;
                }
            }

            if (maxVertex >= 0)
            {
                result.Add(maxVertex);
                remaining.Remove(maxVertex);
            }
            else
            {
                break;
            }
        }

        return result;
    }
}

View C# Code (5942 chars)
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Linq;

class Program
{
    static void Main()
    {
        string[] line = Console.ReadLine().Split();
        int n = int.Parse(line[0]);
        int m = int.Parse(line[1]);

        List<int>[] adj = new List<int>[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
            adj[i] = new List<int>();

        for (int i = 0; i < m; i++)
        {
            string[] edge = Console.ReadLine().Split();
            int u = int.Parse(edge[0]);
            int v = int.Parse(edge[1]);
            adj[u].Add(v);
        }

        List<int> fvs = SolveDFVS(n, adj);

        Console.WriteLine(fvs.Count);
        if (fvs.Count > 0)
            Console.WriteLine(string.Join(" ", fvs));
        else
            Console.WriteLine();
    }

    static List<int> SolveDFVS(int n, List<int>[] adj)
    {
        List<HashSet<int>> sccs = FindSCCs(n, adj);

        HashSet<int> result = new HashSet<int>();

        foreach (var scc in sccs)
        {
            if (scc.Count > 1)
            {
                List<int> fvsForScc = FindMinimalFVSForSCC(scc.ToList(), adj);
                foreach (var v in fvsForScc)
                    result.Add(v);
            }
        }

        return result.OrderBy(x => x).ToList();
    }

    static List<HashSet<int>> FindSCCs(int n, List<int>[] adj)
    {
        bool[] visited = new bool[n];
        Stack<int> finish = new Stack<int>();

        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
        {
            if (!visited[i])
                DFS1(i, adj, visited, finish);
        }

        List<int>[] radj = new List<int>[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
            radj[i] = new List<int>();

        for (int u = 0; u < n; u++)
        {
            foreach (var v in adj[u])
                radj[v].Add(u);
        }

        visited = new bool[n];
        List<HashSet<int>> sccs = new List<HashSet<int>>();

        while (finish.Count > 0)
        {
            int u = finish.Pop();
            if (!visited[u])
            {
                HashSet<int> scc = new HashSet<int>();
                DFS2(u, radj, visited, scc);
                sccs.Add(scc);
            }
        }

        return sccs;
    }

    static void DFS1(int u, List<int>[] adj, bool[] visited, Stack<int> finish)
    {
        visited[u] = true;
        foreach (var v in adj[u])
        {
            if (!visited[v])
                DFS1(v, adj, visited, finish);
        }
        finish.Push(u);
    }

    static void DFS2(int u, List<int>[] radj, bool[] visited, HashSet<int> scc)
    {
        visited[u] = true;
        scc.Add(u);
        foreach (var v in radj[u])
        {
            if (!visited[v])
                DFS2(v, radj, visited, scc);
        }
    }

    static List<int> FindMinimalFVSForSCC(List<int> scc, List<int>[] adj)
    {
        foreach (var v in scc)
        {
            if (IsAcyclicAfterRemoval(scc, new List<int> { v }, adj))
                return new List<int> { v };
        }

        if (scc.Count <= 15)
        {
            for (int i = 0; i < scc.Count; i++)
            {
                for (int j = i + 1; j < scc.Count; j++)
                {
                    var removal = new List<int> { scc[i], scc[j] };
                    if (IsAcyclicAfterRemoval(scc, removal, adj))
                        return removal;
                }
            }
        }

        return GreedyRemoval(scc, adj);
    }

    static bool IsAcyclicAfterRemoval(List<int> scc, List<int> removed, List<int>[] adj)
    {
        HashSet<int> removedSet = new HashSet<int>(removed);
        HashSet<int> sccSet = new HashSet<int>(scc);

        HashSet<int> visited = new HashSet<int>();
        HashSet<int> recStack = new HashSet<int>();

        foreach (var u in scc)
        {
            if (!removedSet.Contains(u) && !visited.Contains(u))
            {
                if (HasCycleDFS(u, adj, sccSet, removedSet, visited, recStack))
                    return false;
            }
        }

        return true;
    }

    static bool HasCycleDFS(int u, List<int>[] adj, HashSet<int> sccSet, HashSet<int> removed, HashSet<int> visited, HashSet<int> recStack)
    {
        visited.Add(u);
        recStack.Add(u);

        foreach (var v in adj[u])
        {
            if (!sccSet.Contains(v) || removed.Contains(v))
                continue;

            if (!visited.Contains(v))
            {
                if (HasCycleDFS(v, adj, sccSet, removed, visited, recStack))
                    return true;
            }
            else if (recStack.Contains(v))
            {
                return true;
            }
        }

        recStack.Remove(u);
        return false;
    }

    static List<int> GreedyRemoval(List<int> scc, List<int>[] adj)
    {
        List<int> result = new List<int>();
        HashSet<int> remaining = new HashSet<int>(scc);

        while (remaining.Count > 0)
        {
            if (IsAcyclicAfterRemoval(remaining.ToList(), new List<int>(), adj))
                break;

            int maxDegree = -1;
            int maxVertex = -1;

            foreach (var u in remaining)
            {
                int degree = 0;
                foreach (var v in adj[u])
                {
                    if (remaining.Contains(v))
                        degree++;
                }
                foreach (var v in remaining)
                {
                    if (v != u && adj[v].Contains(u))
                        degree++;
                }

                if (degree > maxDegree)
                {
                    maxDegree = degree;
                    maxVertex = u;
                }
            }

            if (maxVertex >= 0)
            {
                result.Add(maxVertex);
                remaining.Remove(maxVertex);
            }
            else
            {
                break;
            }
        }

        return result;
    }
}

Test 38: Exact Cover (Python) Score: 0.00 / 16
Typical Prompt:
You are writing Python code to solve the **Exact Cover** problem.

Given a universe of elements and a list of sets, choose set indices such that:
1) every element is covered, and
2) no element is covered more than once.

The evaluator is strict: only the exact expected solution is accepted.

**Input format (stdin)**
```
U S
e1 e2 e3 ...
e1 e2 ...
... (S lines total)
```
- `U` = number of elements in the universe (elements are `0..U-1`)
- `S` = number of available sets
- each following line is one set, listed as space-separated element IDs

**Output format (stdout)**
```
SOLUTION
i1 i2 i3 ... ik
```
or
```
NO SOLUTION
```

- `i1..ik` are 0-based set indices into the input list.

**What is checked**
1. Output format is valid.
2. Indices are valid and distinct.
3. Chosen indices are exactly the planted exact-cover solution.

Write complete Python code that reads stdin and writes stdout.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Given a collection of sets, choose some of them so that every element in the universe appears in exactly one chosen set — no gaps and no overlaps. Think of tiling a floor with pre-cut pieces where every tile must be used exactly once and every square must be covered.

This is a classic NP-complete problem. Each test instance has exactly one valid solution, so grading is strict pass/fail. Subpasses increase the universe and set sizes.

 0.0000
[tiny: universe=40, sets=32] FAIL: Wrong index set (missing: 2, 3, 4, 5; extras: 12, 19, 22, 29)

Exact Cover - tiny: universe=40, sets=32

Universe: 40 elements | Total sets: 32 | Planted sets: 8 (block size 5) | Decoys: 24

Approach: Read input

View Python Code (1027 chars)
line = input().split()
U = int(line[0])
S = int(line[1])

sets = []
for _ in range(S):
    elements = list(map(int, input().split()))
    sets.append(set(elements))

# Backtracking to find exact cover
def backtrack(index, covered, selected):
    # Check if all elements are covered
    if len(covered) == U:
        return selected[:]

    # Pruning: if we've considered all sets and haven't found solution
    if index == S:
        return None

    # Try including the current set if it doesn't overlap
    if not (sets[index] & covered):
        selected.append(index)
        result = backtrack(index + 1, covered | sets[index], selected)
        if result is not None:
            return result
        selected.pop()

    # Try not including the current set
    result = backtrack(index + 1, covered, selected)
    if result is not None:
        return result

    return None

result = backtrack(0, set(), [])

if result is None:
    print("NO SOLUTION")
else:
    print("SOLUTION")
    print(' '.join(map(str, result)))
Set-Element Matrix — Wrong index set (missing: 2, 3, 4, 5; extras: 12, 19, 22, 29)
Universe: 40 | Sets: 32 | Expected picks: 8 | Chosen: 8 | Missing: 4 | Extras: 4
Rows are sets and columns are elements. Green rows are correctly chosen planted sets, red rows are missing planted sets, amber rows are incorrect extra selections.

View raw AI output

 0.0000
[small: universe=80, sets=80] FAIL: Timeout

Exact Cover - small: universe=80, sets=80

Universe: 80 elements | Total sets: 80 | Planted sets: 16 (block size 5) | Decoys: 64

Approach: Read input

View Python Code (1027 chars)
line = input().split()
U = int(line[0])
S = int(line[1])

sets = []
for _ in range(S):
    elements = list(map(int, input().split()))
    sets.append(set(elements))

# Backtracking to find exact cover
def backtrack(index, covered, selected):
    # Check if all elements are covered
    if len(covered) == U:
        return selected[:]

    # Pruning: if we've considered all sets and haven't found solution
    if index == S:
        return None

    # Try including the current set if it doesn't overlap
    if not (sets[index] & covered):
        selected.append(index)
        result = backtrack(index + 1, covered | sets[index], selected)
        if result is not None:
            return result
        selected.pop()

    # Try not including the current set
    result = backtrack(index + 1, covered, selected)
    if result is not None:
        return result

    return None

result = backtrack(0, set(), [])

if result is None:
    print("NO SOLUTION")
else:
    print("SOLUTION")
    print(' '.join(map(str, result)))

Test 39: Graph Bisection (Rust) Score: 0.00 / 13
Typical Prompt:
You are writing Rust code to solve the **Graph Bisection** problem.

Given an undirected graph with an even number of vertices, split the vertices into two equal-size parts to minimize cut edges (edges with endpoints in different parts).

The evaluator is strict: only a minimum-cut valid bisection is accepted.

**Input format (stdin)**
```
n m
u v
u v
... (m lines total)
```
- `n` vertices labeled `0..n-1` (n is even)
- `m` undirected edges

**Output format (stdout)**
```
cut_size
v1 v2 v3 ... v(n/2)
```
- first line: reported cut size
- second line: vertices in partition A (exactly n/2 distinct indices)
- partition B is all remaining vertices

**What is checked**
1. Output format is valid.
2. Partition size is exactly `n/2` and vertices are valid/distinct.
3. Reported cut matches the actual cut.
4. The cut is minimum for the instance.

Write complete Rust code with `main` that reads stdin and writes stdout.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Split a graph’s nodes into two equally-sized groups so that as few edges as possible cross between the groups. Think of dividing a school into two teams where friends want to stay together — minimise the number of friendships that get split.

The test uses planted graphs that can be perfectly bisected with zero crossing edges, so grading is strict pass/fail. Subpasses increase the graph size.

 0.0000
[tiny: 40 vertices] FAIL: Cut is 49, but optimum is 0

Graph Bisection - tiny: 40 vertices

Vertices: 40 | Edges: ~108 | Target partition size: 20 | Known optimum cut: 0

View Rust Code (4542 chars)
use std::io::{self, BufRead};
use std::collections::HashSet;

fn main() {
    let stdin = io::stdin();
    let mut lines = stdin.lock().lines();

    // Parse input
    let first_line = lines.next().unwrap().unwrap();
    let parts: Vec<usize> = first_line.split_whitespace()
        .map(|s| s.parse().unwrap())
        .collect();
    let n = parts[0];
    let m = parts[1];

    // Build adjacency list
    let mut adj = vec![Vec::new(); n];
    for _ in 0..m {
        let line = lines.next().unwrap().unwrap();
        let edge: Vec<usize> = line.split_whitespace()
            .map(|s| s.parse().unwrap())
            .collect();
        let u = edge[0];
        let v = edge[1];
        adj[u].push(v);
        adj[v].push(u);
    }

    // Find best partition using multiple random restarts and local search
    let mut best_partition = Vec::new();
    let mut best_cut = usize::MAX;

    for seed in 0..100 {
        let partition = make_initial_partition(n, seed);
        let partition = local_search(partition, &adj, n);
        let cut = count_cut(&partition, &adj);

        if cut < best_cut {
            best_cut = cut;
            best_partition = partition;
        }
    }

    // Output result
    println!("{}", best_cut);
    let output = best_partition.iter()
        .map(|x| x.to_string())
        .collect::<Vec<_>>()
        .join(" ");
    println!("{}", output);
}

fn make_initial_partition(n: usize, seed: usize) -> Vec<usize> {
    // Create pseudo-random initial partition
    let mut partition = Vec::new();
    let mut used = vec![false; n];

    // Use a simple hash to select vertices
    for i in 0..n {
        if partition.len() < n / 2 {
            let hash = ((i as u64).wrapping_mul(73856093) ^ (seed as u64).wrapping_mul(19349663)) as usize;
            if (hash % 2) == 0 {
                partition.push(i);
                used[i] = true;
            }
        }
    }

    // Fill remaining slots
    for i in 0..n {
        if partition.len() < n / 2 && !used[i] {
            partition.push(i);
        }
    }

    partition
}

fn local_search(mut partition: Vec<usize>, adj: &[Vec<usize>], n: usize) -> Vec<usize> {
    let mut improved = true;
    let mut iterations = 0;

    while improved && iterations < 5000 {
        improved = false;
        iterations += 1;

        let set_a: HashSet<usize> = partition.iter().copied().collect();

        // Try swapping each vertex in partition A with each in B
        for i_idx in 0..partition.len() {
            let i_vertex = partition[i_idx];

            for j_vertex in 0..n {
                if set_a.contains(&j_vertex) {
                    continue;
                }

                // Calculate the change in cut size if we swap i_vertex and j_vertex
                let delta = calculate_swap_delta(i_vertex, j_vertex, &set_a, adj);

                if delta < 0 {
                    // Swap improves the cut
                    partition[i_idx] = j_vertex;
                    improved = true;
                    break;
                }
            }

            if improved {
                break;
            }
        }
    }

    partition
}

fn calculate_swap_delta(i_vertex: usize, j_vertex: usize, set_a: &HashSet<usize>, adj: &[Vec<usize>]) -> i32 {
    let mut delta = 0i32;

    // Count edges from i_vertex
    for &neighbor in &adj[i_vertex] {
        if set_a.contains(&neighbor) && neighbor != i_vertex {
            // Internal edge: removing i breaks it
            delta += 1;
        } else if !set_a.contains(&neighbor) {
            // External edge: removing i loses it from cut
            delta -= 1;
        }
    }

    // Count edges from j_vertex
    for &neighbor in &adj[j_vertex] {
        if set_a.contains(&neighbor) {
            // Edge to partition A: adding j creates cut edge
            delta += 1;
        } else if !set_a.contains(&neighbor) && neighbor != j_vertex {
            // Internal edge: adding j breaks it
            delta -= 1;
        }
    }

    // Handle direct edge between i_vertex and j_vertex
    if adj[i_vertex].contains(&j_vertex) {
        // This edge becomes internal, reduces cut by 1
        delta -= 1;
    }

    delta
}

fn count_cut(partition: &[usize], adj: &[Vec<usize>]) -> usize {
    let set_a: HashSet<usize> = partition.iter().copied().collect();
    let mut cut = 0;

    for &v in partition {
        for &neighbor in &adj[v] {
            if !set_a.contains(&neighbor) {
                cut += 1;
            }
        }
    }

    cut
}

View Rust Code (4542 chars)
use std::io::{self, BufRead};
use std::collections::HashSet;

fn main() {
    let stdin = io::stdin();
    let mut lines = stdin.lock().lines();

    // Parse input
    let first_line = lines.next().unwrap().unwrap();
    let parts: Vec<usize> = first_line.split_whitespace()
        .map(|s| s.parse().unwrap())
        .collect();
    let n = parts[0];
    let m = parts[1];

    // Build adjacency list
    let mut adj = vec![Vec::new(); n];
    for _ in 0..m {
        let line = lines.next().unwrap().unwrap();
        let edge: Vec<usize> = line.split_whitespace()
            .map(|s| s.parse().unwrap())
            .collect();
        let u = edge[0];
        let v = edge[1];
        adj[u].push(v);
        adj[v].push(u);
    }

    // Find best partition using multiple random restarts and local search
    let mut best_partition = Vec::new();
    let mut best_cut = usize::MAX;

    for seed in 0..100 {
        let partition = make_initial_partition(n, seed);
        let partition = local_search(partition, &adj, n);
        let cut = count_cut(&partition, &adj);

        if cut < best_cut {
            best_cut = cut;
            best_partition = partition;
        }
    }

    // Output result
    println!("{}", best_cut);
    let output = best_partition.iter()
        .map(|x| x.to_string())
        .collect::<Vec<_>>()
        .join(" ");
    println!("{}", output);
}

fn make_initial_partition(n: usize, seed: usize) -> Vec<usize> {
    // Create pseudo-random initial partition
    let mut partition = Vec::new();
    let mut used = vec![false; n];

    // Use a simple hash to select vertices
    for i in 0..n {
        if partition.len() < n / 2 {
            let hash = ((i as u64).wrapping_mul(73856093) ^ (seed as u64).wrapping_mul(19349663)) as usize;
            if (hash % 2) == 0 {
                partition.push(i);
                used[i] = true;
            }
        }
    }

    // Fill remaining slots
    for i in 0..n {
        if partition.len() < n / 2 && !used[i] {
            partition.push(i);
        }
    }

    partition
}

fn local_search(mut partition: Vec<usize>, adj: &[Vec<usize>], n: usize) -> Vec<usize> {
    let mut improved = true;
    let mut iterations = 0;

    while improved && iterations < 5000 {
        improved = false;
        iterations += 1;

        let set_a: HashSet<usize> = partition.iter().copied().collect();

        // Try swapping each vertex in partition A with each in B
        for i_idx in 0..partition.len() {
            let i_vertex = partition[i_idx];

            for j_vertex in 0..n {
                if set_a.contains(&j_vertex) {
                    continue;
                }

                // Calculate the change in cut size if we swap i_vertex and j_vertex
                let delta = calculate_swap_delta(i_vertex, j_vertex, &set_a, adj);

                if delta < 0 {
                    // Swap improves the cut
                    partition[i_idx] = j_vertex;
                    improved = true;
                    break;
                }
            }

            if improved {
                break;
            }
        }
    }

    partition
}

fn calculate_swap_delta(i_vertex: usize, j_vertex: usize, set_a: &HashSet<usize>, adj: &[Vec<usize>]) -> i32 {
    let mut delta = 0i32;

    // Count edges from i_vertex
    for &neighbor in &adj[i_vertex] {
        if set_a.contains(&neighbor) && neighbor != i_vertex {
            // Internal edge: removing i breaks it
            delta += 1;
        } else if !set_a.contains(&neighbor) {
            // External edge: removing i loses it from cut
            delta -= 1;
        }
    }

    // Count edges from j_vertex
    for &neighbor in &adj[j_vertex] {
        if set_a.contains(&neighbor) {
            // Edge to partition A: adding j creates cut edge
            delta += 1;
        } else if !set_a.contains(&neighbor) && neighbor != j_vertex {
            // Internal edge: adding j breaks it
            delta -= 1;
        }
    }

    // Handle direct edge between i_vertex and j_vertex
    if adj[i_vertex].contains(&j_vertex) {
        // This edge becomes internal, reduces cut by 1
        delta -= 1;
    }

    delta
}

fn count_cut(partition: &[usize], adj: &[Vec<usize>]) -> usize {
    let set_a: HashSet<usize> = partition.iter().copied().collect();
    let mut cut = 0;

    for &v in partition {
        for &neighbor in &adj[v] {
            if !set_a.contains(&neighbor) {
                cut += 1;
            }
        }
    }

    cut
}

Test 40: Integer Linear Programming (C++) Score: 1.00 / 14
Typical Prompt:
You are writing C++ code to solve a **0/1 Integer Linear Program**.

Maximize:
sum(c_i * x_i)

Subject to:
x_u + x_v <= 1 (for each constraint pair u, v)
x_i in {0, 1}

**Input format (stdin)**
```
n m
c_0 c_1 ... c_(n-1)
u_0 v_0
u_1 v_1
... (m lines total)
```
- `n` = number of binary variables
- `m` = number of pair constraints

**Output format (stdout)**
```
objective_value
x_0 x_1 ... x_(n-1)
```

**What is checked**
1. Output format is valid.
2. `x_i` are binary and satisfy all constraints.
3. Reported objective matches the assignment.
4. Assignment achieves the true optimum for the instance.

Write complete C++17 code with `main()` that reads stdin and writes stdout.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Maximise a score by choosing which items to switch “on” or “off”, subject to a set of rules that restrict certain combinations. Each item has a value, and the rules say things like “you can’t pick both A and B”.

This is 0/1 Integer Linear Programming, a fundamental NP-hard optimisation problem. The test uses planted instances with a known unique optimum for strict pass/fail grading. Subpasses increase the number of variables and constraints.

 1.0000
[20 vars, 10 constraints] PASS: objective 133 (optimal) in 0.17s

Integer Linear Programming - 20 vars, 10 constraints

Binary variables: 20 | Pair constraints: 10 | Planted optimum is unique

View C++ Code (1498 chars)
#include <iostream>
#include <vector>
#include <algorithm>

using namespace std;

int main() {
    ios_base::sync_with_stdio(false);
    cin.tie(nullptr);

    int n, m;
    cin >> n >> m;

    vector<long long> c(n);
    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
        cin >> c[i];
    }

    vector<pair<int, int>> constraints(m);
    for (int i = 0; i < m; i++) {
        cin >> constraints[i].first >> constraints[i].second;
    }

    long long best_objective = 0;
    vector<int> best_solution(n, 0);

    // Try all 2^n possible assignments
    for (long long mask = 0; mask < (1LL << n); mask++) {
        // Check constraints
        bool valid = true;
        for (const auto& [u, v] : constraints) {
            int xu = (mask >> u) & 1;
            int xv = (mask >> v) & 1;
            if (xu + xv > 1) {
                valid = false;
                break;
            }
        }

        if (!valid) continue;

        // Calculate objective
        long long objective = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            if ((mask >> i) & 1) {
                objective += c[i];
            }
        }

        if (objective > best_objective) {
            best_objective = objective;
            for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
                best_solution[i] = (mask >> i) & 1;
            }
        }
    }

    cout << best_objective << "\n";
    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
        if (i > 0) cout << " ";
        cout << best_solution[i];
    }
    cout << "\n";

    return 0;
}
Constraint Pair Visualization — Valid optimal assignment
Pairs: 10 | Reported obj: 133 | Actual obj: 133 | Optimum: 133 | Mismatched vars: 0 | Violated pairs: 0
Green nodes match planted optimum (selected). Gray are correctly unselected. Amber/Red indicate wrong variable decisions. Red pair lines mark violated constraints.

View C++ Code (1498 chars)
#include <iostream>
#include <vector>
#include <algorithm>

using namespace std;

int main() {
    ios_base::sync_with_stdio(false);
    cin.tie(nullptr);

    int n, m;
    cin >> n >> m;

    vector<long long> c(n);
    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
        cin >> c[i];
    }

    vector<pair<int, int>> constraints(m);
    for (int i = 0; i < m; i++) {
        cin >> constraints[i].first >> constraints[i].second;
    }

    long long best_objective = 0;
    vector<int> best_solution(n, 0);

    // Try all 2^n possible assignments
    for (long long mask = 0; mask < (1LL << n); mask++) {
        // Check constraints
        bool valid = true;
        for (const auto& [u, v] : constraints) {
            int xu = (mask >> u) & 1;
            int xv = (mask >> v) & 1;
            if (xu + xv > 1) {
                valid = false;
                break;
            }
        }

        if (!valid) continue;

        // Calculate objective
        long long objective = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            if ((mask >> i) & 1) {
                objective += c[i];
            }
        }

        if (objective > best_objective) {
            best_objective = objective;
            for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
                best_solution[i] = (mask >> i) & 1;
            }
        }
    }

    cout << best_objective << "\n";
    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
        if (i > 0) cout << " ";
        cout << best_solution[i];
    }
    cout << "\n";

    return 0;
}

Test 41: Fragment Shaders (SPIR-V Assembly) Score: 0.00 / 20
Typical Prompt:
You are writing a SPIR-V assembly text fragment shader. The shader will be assembled
with spirv-as (Vulkan 1.0 target) and rendered on a unit sphere.

**Available inputs (from vertex shader):**
```
layout(location = 0) in vec3 worldPos; ; world-space position
layout(location = 1) in vec3 normal; ; world-space normal (interpolated, may need normalize)
layout(location = 2) in vec2 uv; ; texture coordinates in [0,1]
layout(location = 3) in vec3 tangent; ; tangent vector
layout(location = 4) in vec3 color; ; per-vertex color (smoothly varies: typical value: rgb = 0.5+0.5*xyz)
```

**Available uniform buffer (DescriptorSet 0, Binding 0):**
```
struct UBO { // std140 layout
mat4 model; // offset 0 (column-major)
mat4 view; // offset 64
mat4 projection; // offset 128
vec4 lightPos; // offset 192 -- typical value: (5.0, 5.0, 5.0, 1.0)
vec4 cameraPos; // offset 208 -- typical value: (0.0, 0.0, 3.0, 1.0)
vec4 params; // offset 224 -- typical value: (0.0, 0.5, 0.0, 0.0)
};
```

**Required output:**
```
layout(location = 0) out vec4 outColor; RGBA float, will be stored as rgba8unorm
```

**SPIR-V requirements:**
- Start with `; SPIR-V` header comment and version
- Use `OpCapability Shader` and `OpMemoryModel Logical GLSL450`
- Use `OpEntryPoint Fragment %main "main"` listing ALL input/output variables used
- Use `OpExecutionMode %main OriginUpperLeft`
- Declare all necessary decorations (Location, DescriptorSet, Binding, Block, Offsets)
- All input variables are `Input` storage class, output is `Output` storage class
- The uniform buffer variable is `Uniform` storage class
- Entry point function name must be "main"

**Write ONLY the complete SPIR-V assembly text.** Do not include any other text or explanation
outside the SPIR-V text.


**Task:** Output a solid red color (vec4(1.0, 0.0, 0.0, 1.0)).
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Write GPU fragment shaders in raw SPIR-V assembly — the low-level instruction set that graphics cards actually execute. Each subpass asks for a different visual effect (lighting, patterns, fractals) rendered onto a 3D sphere, and the output image is compared pixel-by-pixel against a reference.

This is the hardest shader-language test because SPIR-V assembly is essentially GPU machine code: every operation, type declaration, and memory access must be spelled out by hand.

 0.0000
[Solid Red] SPIR-V validation failed: error: line 14: Constant cannot appear in a function declaration
%float_1 = OpConstant %float 1

SPIR-V Shader - Solid Red

View SPIR-V (682 chars)
; SPIR-V
; Version: 1.0
; Generator: Hand-written
; Bound: 20
; Schema: 0

OpCapability Shader
OpMemoryModel Logical GLSL450

OpEntryPoint Fragment %main "main" %outColor
OpExecutionMode %main OriginUpperLeft

OpDecorate %outColor Location 0

%void = OpTypeVoid
%float = OpTypeFloat 32
%vec4 = OpTypeVector %float 4

%outColor_ptr = OpTypePointer Output %vec4
%outColor = OpVariable %outColor_ptr Output

%main_type = OpTypeFunction %void

%main = OpFunction %void None %main_type
%entry = OpLabel

%const_1 = OpConstant %float 1.0
%const_0 = OpConstant %float 0.0
%red = OpCompositeConstruct %vec4 %const_1 %const_0 %const_0 %const_1

OpStore %outColor %red

OpReturn
OpFunctionEnd

SPIR-V Shader - Normal to RGB

View SPIR-V (1525 chars)
; SPIR-V
; Version: 1.0
; Generator: Custom
; Bound: 80
; Schema: 0
OpCapability Shader
%glsl = OpExtInstImport "GLSL.std.450"
OpMemoryModel Logical GLSL450
OpEntryPoint Fragment %main "main" %in_normal %out_color
OpExecutionMode %main OriginUpperLeft
OpDecorate %in_normal Location 1
OpDecorate %out_color Location 0

; Type declarations
%void = OpTypeVoid
%float = OpTypeFloat 32
%v3float = OpTypeVector %float 3
%v4float = OpTypeVector %float 4
%ptr_Input_v3float = OpTypePointer Input %v3float
%ptr_Output_v4float = OpTypePointer Output %v4float
%fn_type = OpTypeFunction %void

; Constant declarations
%float_0_5 = OpConstant %float 0.5
%float_1_0 = OpConstant %float 1.0
%v3_1_0 = OpConstantComposite %v3float %float_1_0 %float_1_0 %float_1_0
%v3_0_5 = OpConstantComposite %v3float %float_0_5 %float_0_5 %float_0_5

; Variable declarations
%in_normal = OpVariable %ptr_Input_v3float Input
%out_color = OpVariable %ptr_Output_v4float Output

; Main function
%main = OpFunction %void None %fn_type
%entry_block = OpLabel

; Load the normal vector
%normal_loaded = OpLoad %v3float %in_normal

; Normalize the normal
%normalized = OpExtInst %v3float %glsl Normalize %normal_loaded

; Map from [-1, 1] to [0, 1] using formula: (n + 1.0) * 0.5
%shifted = OpFAdd %v3float %normalized %v3_1_0
%mapped = OpFMul %v3float %shifted %v3_0_5

; Construct output color with alpha = 1.0
%final_color = OpCompositeConstruct %v4float %mapped %float_1_0

; Store the output
OpStore %out_color %final_color

; Return
OpReturn
OpFunctionEnd

OpCapability Shader
OpMemoryModel Logical GLSL450
OpEntryPoint Fragment %main "main" %in_uv %out_color
OpExecutionMode %main OriginUpperLeft

OpDecorate %in_uv Location 2
OpDecorate %out_color Location 0

OpDecorate %ubo_block Block
OpDecorate %ubo_var DescriptorSet 0
OpDecorate %ubo_var Binding 0
OpMemberDecorate %ubo_block 0 Offset 0
OpMemberDecorate %ubo_block 1 Offset 64
OpMemberDecorate %ubo_block 2 Offset 128
OpMemberDecorate %ubo_block 3 Offset 192
OpMemberDecorate %ubo_block 4 Offset 208
OpMemberDecorate %ubo_block 5 Offset 224

%void = OpTypeVoid
%float = OpTypeFloat 32
%v2float = OpTypeVector %float 2
%v4float = OpTypeVector %float 4
%mat4 = OpTypeMatrix %v4float 4

%ubo_block = OpTypeStruct %mat4 %mat4 %mat4 %v4float %v4float %v4float
%ubo_ptr = OpTypePointer Uniform %ubo_block
%in_ptr_v2 = OpTypePointer Input %v2float
%out_ptr_v4 = OpTypePointer Output %v4float

%in_uv = OpVariable %in_ptr_v2 Input
%out_color = OpVariable %out_ptr_v4 Output
%ubo_var = OpVariable %ubo_ptr Uniform

%func_type = OpTypeFunction %void
%main = OpFunction %void None %func_type
%entry = OpLabel

%uv = OpLoad %v2float %in_uv
%uv_x = OpCompositeExtract %float %uv 0
%uv_y = OpCompositeExtract %float %uv 1
%zero = OpConstant %float 0.0
%one = OpConstant %float 1.0
%color = OpCompositeConstruct %v4float %uv_x %uv_y %zero %one

OpStore %out_color %color
OpReturn
OpFunctionEnd

; Version: 1.0
; Generator: Manual; 11

OpCapability Shader
OpMemoryModel Logical GLSL450
OpEntryPoint Fragment %main "main" %in_color %out_color
OpExecutionMode %main OriginUpperLeft
OpDecorate %out_color Location 0
OpDecorate %in_color Location 4
%void = OpTypeVoid
%float = OpTypeFloat 32
%v3float = OpTypeVector %float 3
%v4float = OpTypeVector %float 4
%float_1 = OpConstant %float 1.0
%ptr_Input_v3float = OpTypePointer Input %v3float
%ptr_Output_v4float = OpTypePointer Output %v4float
%in_color = OpVariable %ptr_Input_v3float Input
%out_color = OpVariable %ptr_Output_v4float Output
%void_fn = OpTypeFunction %void
%main = OpFunction %void None %void_fn
%label = OpLabel
%color_val = OpLoad %v3float %in_color
%comp0 = OpCompositeExtract %float %color_val 0
%comp1 = OpCompositeExtract %float %color_val 1
%comp2 = OpCompositeExtract %float %color_val 2
%final = OpCompositeConstruct %v4float %comp0 %comp1 %comp2 %float_1
OpStore %out_color %final
OpReturn
OpFunctionEnd

OpCapability Shader
OpExtInstImport "GLSL.std.450" %glsl
OpMemoryModel Logical GLSL450
OpEntryPoint Fragment %main "main" %in_worldPos %in_normal %in_uv %in_tangent %in_color %out_color
OpExecutionMode %main OriginUpperLeft
OpDecorate %in_worldPos Location 0
OpDecorate %in_normal Location 1
OpDecorate %in_uv Location 2
OpDecorate %in_tangent Location 3
OpDecorate %in_color Location 4
OpDecorate %out_color Location 0
OpDecorate %UBO Block
OpDecorate %ubo_var DescriptorSet 0
OpDecorate %ubo_var Binding 0
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 0 Offset 0
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 1 Offset 64
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 2 Offset 128
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 3 Offset 192
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 4 Offset 208
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 5 Offset 224

%void = OpTypeVoid
%float = OpTypeFloat 32
%vec2 = OpTypeVector %float 2
%vec3 = OpTypeVector %float 3
%vec4 = OpTypeVector %float 4
%mat4 = OpTypeMatrix %vec4 4

%UBO = OpTypeStruct %mat4 %mat4 %mat4 %vec4 %vec4 %vec4

%ptr_Uniform_UBO = OpTypePointer Uniform %UBO
%ptr_Uniform_vec4 = OpTypePointer Uniform %vec4
%ptr_Input_vec3 = OpTypePointer Input %vec3
%ptr_Input_vec2 = OpTypePointer Input %vec2
%ptr_Output_vec4 = OpTypePointer Output %vec4

%ubo_var = OpVariable %ptr_Uniform_UBO Uniform
%in_worldPos = OpVariable %ptr_Input_vec3 Input
%in_normal = OpVariable %ptr_Input_vec3 Input
%in_uv = OpVariable %ptr_Input_vec2 Input
%in_tangent = OpVariable %ptr_Input_vec3 Input
%in_color = OpVariable %ptr_Input_vec3 Input
%out_color = OpVariable %ptr_Output_vec4 Output

%uint = OpTypeInt 32 0
%uint_3 = OpConstant %uint 3
%float_0 = OpConstant %float 0.0
%float_1 = OpConstant %float 1.0

%func_type = OpTypeFunction %void

%main = OpFunction %void None %func_type
%entry = OpLabel

%normal = OpLoad %vec3 %in_normal
%worldPos = OpLoad %vec3 %in_worldPos

%light_ptr = OpAccessChain %ptr_Uniform_vec4 %ubo_var %uint_3
%light_vec4 = OpLoad %vec4 %light_ptr
%lightPos = OpVectorShuffle %vec3 %light_vec4 %light_vec4 0 1 2

%L_unnorm = OpFSub %vec3 %lightPos %worldPos
%L = OpExtInst %vec3 %glsl Normalize %L_unnorm

%N = OpExtInst %vec3 %glsl Normalize %normal

%dot_NL = OpDot %float %N %L

%d = OpExtInst %float %glsl FMax %dot_NL %float_0

%result = OpCompositeConstruct %vec4 %d %d %d %float_1

OpStore %out_color %result

OpReturn
OpFunctionEnd

OpCapability Shader
OpMemoryModel Logical GLSL450
OpEntryPoint Fragment %main "main" %worldPos %normal %uv %tangent %color %outColor
OpExecutionMode %main OriginUpperLeft
OpDecorate %worldPos Location 0
OpDecorate %normal Location 1
OpDecorate %uv Location 2
OpDecorate %tangent Location 3
OpDecorate %color Location 4
OpDecorate %outColor Location 0

OpDecorate %ubo Binding 0

OpMemberDecorate %UBO 0 Offset 0
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 0 ColMajor
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 0 MatrixStride 16
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 1 Offset 64
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 1 ColMajor
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 1 MatrixStride 16
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 2 Offset 128
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 2 ColMajor
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 2 MatrixStride 16
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 3 Offset 192
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 4 Offset 208
OpMemberDecorate %UBO 5 Offset 224
%void = OpTypeVoid
%bool = OpTypeBool
%int = OpTypeInt 32 1
%float = OpTypeFloat 32
%vec2 = OpTypeVector %float 2
%vec3 = OpTypeVector %float 3
%vec4 = OpTypeVector %float 4
%mat4 = OpTypeMatrix %vec4 4
%UBO = OpTypeStruct %mat4 %mat4 %mat4 %vec4 %vec4 %vec4
%ptr_Uniform_UBO = OpTypePointer Uniform %UBO
%ptr_Input_vec3 = OpTypePointer Input %vec3
%ptr_Input_vec2 = OpTypePointer Input %vec2
%ptr_Output_vec4 = OpTypePointer Output %vec4
%ptr_Uniform_vec4 = OpTypePointer Uniform %vec4
%ptr_Function_vec3 = OpTypePointer Function %vec3
%ptr_Function_vec4 = OpTypePointer Function %vec4
%ptr_Function_float = OpTypePointer Function %float
%void_fn = OpTypeFunction %void
%ubo = OpVariable %ptr_Uniform_UBO Uniform
%worldPos = OpVariable %ptr_Input_vec3 Input
%normal = OpVariable %ptr_Input_vec3 Input
%uv = OpVariable %ptr_Input_vec2 Input
%tangent = OpVariable %ptr_Input_vec3 Input
%color = OpVariable %ptr_Input_vec3 Input
%outColor = OpVariable %ptr_Output_vec4 Output
%c_zero = OpConstant %float 0.0
%c_one = OpConstant %float 1.0
%c_02 = OpConstant %float 0.2
%c_05 = OpConstant %float 0.5
%c_08 = OpConstant %float 0.8
%c_32 = OpConstant %float 32.0
%c_int_0 = OpConstant %int 0
%c_int_3 = OpConstant %int 3
%c_int_4 = OpConstant %int 4
%glsl = OpExtInstImport "GLSL.std.450"
%main = OpFunction %void None %void_fn
%entry = OpLabel
%wpos = OpLoad %vec3 %worldPos
%norm = OpLoad %vec3 %normal
%tang = OpLoad %vec3 %tangent
%col = OpLoad %vec3 %color
%N = OpExtInst %vec3 %glsl Normalize %norm
%lightPos_ptr = OpAccessChain %ptr_Uniform_vec4 %ubo %c_int_3
%lightPos_v4 = OpLoad %vec4 %lightPos_ptr
%lightPos = OpVectorShuffle %vec3 %lightPos_v4 %lightPos_v4 0 1 2
%camPos_ptr = OpAccessChain %ptr_Uniform_vec4 %ubo %c_int_4
%camPos_v4 = OpLoad %vec4 %camPos_ptr
%camPos = OpVectorShuffle %vec3 %camPos_v4 %camPos_v4 0 1 2
%L_dir = OpFSub %vec3 %lightPos %wpos
%L = OpExtInst %vec3 %glsl Normalize %L_dir
%V_dir = OpFSub %vec3 %camPos %wpos
%V = OpExtInst %vec3 %glsl Normalize %V_dir
%negL = OpFNegate %vec3 %L
%R = OpExtInst %vec3 %glsl Reflect %negL %N
%dotNL = OpDot %float %N %L
%diffuse = OpExtInst %float %glsl FMax %dotNL %c_zero
%dotRV = OpDot %float %R %V
%maxRV = OpExtInst %float %glsl FMax %dotRV %c_zero
%specular = OpExtInst %float %glsl Pow %maxRV %c_32
%d_term = OpFMul %float %c_05 %diffuse
%ds_sum = OpFAdd %float %c_02 %d_term
%s_term = OpFMul %float %c_08 %specular
%intensity = OpFAdd %float %ds_sum %s_term
%result = OpCompositeConstruct %vec4 %intensity %intensity %intensity %c_one
OpStore %outColor %result
OpReturn
OpFunctionEnd

View SPIR-V (4212 chars)
; SPIR-V
; Version: 1.0
; Generator: Custom
; Bound: 250
; Schema: 0
OpCapability Shader
OpExtInstImport "GLSL.std.450"
OpMemoryModel Logical GLSL450
OpEntryPoint Fragment %main "main" %inWorldPos %inNormal %inUV %inTangent %inColor %outColor
OpExecutionMode %main OriginUpperLeft

; Decorations for inputs
OpDecorate %inWorldPos Location 0
OpDecorate %inNormal Location 1
OpDecorate %inUV Location 2
OpDecorate %inTangent Location 3
OpDecorate %inColor Location 4

; Decorations for output
OpDecorate %outColor Location 0

; Decorations for UBO
OpDecorate %ubo Block
OpDecorate %uboInstance DescriptorSet 0
OpDecorate %uboInstance Binding 0

; Member offsets for UBO
OpMemberDecorate %ubo 0 Offset 0
OpMemberDecorate %ubo 1 Offset 64
OpMemberDecorate %ubo 2 Offset 128
OpMemberDecorate %ubo 3 Offset 192
OpMemberDecorate %ubo 4 Offset 208
OpMemberDecorate %ubo 5 Offset 224

; Type declarations
%void = OpTypeVoid
%int = OpTypeInt 32 1
%float = OpTypeFloat 32
%vec2 = OpTypeVector %float 2
%vec3 = OpTypeVector %float 3
%vec4 = OpTypeVector %float 4
%mat4 = OpTypeMatrix %vec4 4

; UBO struct type
%ubo = OpTypeStruct %mat4 %mat4 %mat4 %vec4 %vec4 %vec4

; Pointer types
%ptr_Uniform_ubo = OpTypePointer Uniform %ubo
%ptr_Uniform_vec4 = OpTypePointer Uniform %vec4
%ptr_Input_vec3 = OpTypePointer Input %vec3
%ptr_Input_vec2 = OpTypePointer Input %vec2
%ptr_Output_vec4 = OpTypePointer Output %vec4

; Function type
%void_fn = OpTypeFunction %void

; Integer constants
%int_0 = OpConstant %int 0
%int_3 = OpConstant %int 3
%int_4 = OpConstant %int 4

; Float constants
%float_0 = OpConstant %float 0.0
%float_1 = OpConstant %float 1.0
%float_0_1 = OpConstant %float 0.1
%float_0_6 = OpConstant %float 0.6
%float_0_8 = OpConstant %float 0.8
%float_64 = OpConstant %float 64.0

; Variable declarations
%inWorldPos = OpVariable %ptr_Input_vec3 Input
%inNormal = OpVariable %ptr_Input_vec3 Input
%inUV = OpVariable %ptr_Input_vec2 Input
%inTangent = OpVariable %ptr_Input_vec3 Input
%inColor = OpVariable %ptr_Input_vec3 Input
%outColor = OpVariable %ptr_Output_vec4 Output
%uboInstance = OpVariable %ptr_Uniform_ubo Uniform

; Extended instruction set import
%glsl = OpExtInstImport "GLSL.std.450"

; Main function definition
%main = OpFunction %void None %void_fn
%entry = OpLabel

; Load input values
%worldPos = OpLoad %vec3 %inWorldPos
%normal = OpLoad %vec3 %inNormal
%uv = OpLoad %vec2 %inUV
%tangent = OpLoad %vec3 %inTangent
%color = OpLoad %vec3 %inColor

; Normalize the normal vector
%N = OpExtInst %vec3 %glsl Normalize %normal

; Access lightPos from UBO (member 3)
%lightPosPtr = OpAccessChain %ptr_Uniform_vec4 %uboInstance %int_3
%lightPosVec4 = OpLoad %vec4 %lightPosPtr
%lightPos = OpVectorShuffle %vec3 %lightPosVec4 %lightPosVec4 0 1 2

; Access cameraPos from UBO (member 4)
%cameraPosPtr = OpAccessChain %ptr_Uniform_vec4 %uboInstance %int_4
%cameraPosVec4 = OpLoad %vec4 %cameraPosPtr
%cameraPos = OpVectorShuffle %vec3 %cameraPosVec4 %cameraPosVec4 0 1 2

; Calculate L (normalized light direction)
%lightDir = OpFSub %vec3 %lightPos %worldPos
%L = OpExtInst %vec3 %glsl Normalize %lightDir

; Calculate V (normalized view direction)
%viewDir = OpFSub %vec3 %cameraPos %worldPos
%V = OpExtInst %vec3 %glsl Normalize %viewDir

; Calculate H (half-vector): normalize(L + V)
%LplusV = OpFAdd %vec3 %L %V
%H = OpExtInst %vec3 %glsl Normalize %LplusV

; Calculate diffuse component: max(dot(N, L), 0)
%dotNL = OpDot %float %N %L
%diffuse = OpExtInst %float %glsl FMax %dotNL %float_0

; Calculate specular component: pow(max(dot(N, H), 0), 64)
%dotNH = OpDot %float %N %H
%maxNH = OpExtInst %float %glsl FMax %dotNH %float_0
%specular = OpExtInst %float %glsl Pow %maxNH %float_64

; Calculate final intensity: 0.1 + 0.6*diffuse + 0.8*specular
%diffuseTerm = OpFMul %float %float_0_6 %diffuse
%temp1 = OpFAdd %float %float_0_1 %diffuseTerm
%specularTerm = OpFMul %float %float_0_8 %specular
%intensity = OpFAdd %float %temp1 %specularTerm

; Construct output color as grayscale: (intensity, intensity, intensity, 1.0)
%outColorValue = OpCompositeConstruct %vec4 %intensity %intensity %intensity %float_1

; Store the result
OpStore %outColor %outColorValue

; End function
OpReturn
OpFunctionEnd

; Version: 1.0

OpCapability Shader
OpMemoryModel Logical GLSL450

OpDecorate %in_color Location 4

OpMemberDecorate %ubo_block 4 Offset 208
OpMemberDecorate %ubo_block 5 Offset 224

%void = OpTypeVoid
%float = OpTypeFloat 32
%int = OpTypeInt 32 1
%vec3 = OpTypeVector %float 3
%vec4 = OpTypeVector %float 4
%mat4 = OpTypeMatrix %vec4 4

%ubo_block = OpTypeStruct %mat4 %mat4 %mat4 %vec4 %vec4 %vec4
%ubo_ptr_type = OpTypePointer Uniform %ubo_block
%vec3_input_ptr = OpTypePointer Input %vec3
%vec4_output_ptr = OpTypePointer Output %vec4
%vec4_uniform_ptr = OpTypePointer Uniform %vec4

%main_func_type = OpTypeFunction %void

%float_0 = OpConstant %float 0.0
%float_1 = OpConstant %float 1.0
%float_3 = OpConstant %float 3.0
%float_0p2 = OpConstant %float 0.2
%float_0p3 = OpConstant %float 0.3
%float_0p5 = OpConstant %float 0.5
%float_0p8 = OpConstant %float 0.8
%int_4 = OpConstant %int 4

%base_color = OpConstantComposite %vec3 %float_0p2 %float_0p3 %float_0p8
%rim_tint_color = OpConstantComposite %vec3 %float_1 %float_0p8 %float_0p5
%light_direction = OpConstantComposite %vec3 %float_1 %float_1 %float_1

%ubo_var = OpVariable %ubo_ptr_type Uniform
%in_worldPos = OpVariable %vec3_input_ptr Input
%in_normal = OpVariable %vec3_input_ptr Input
%in_color = OpVariable %vec3_input_ptr Input
%out_color = OpVariable %vec4_output_ptr Output

%glsl_std = OpExtInstImport "GLSL.std.450"

%main = OpFunction %void None %main_func_type
%entry_label = OpLabel

%worldPos = OpLoad %vec3 %in_worldPos
%normal_raw = OpLoad %vec3 %in_normal
%color_unused = OpLoad %vec3 %in_color

%camera_access = OpAccessChain %vec4_uniform_ptr %ubo_var %int_4
%camera_vec4 = OpLoad %vec4 %camera_access
%cameraPos = OpVectorShuffle %vec3 %camera_vec4 %camera_vec4 0 1 2

%to_camera = OpFSub %vec3 %cameraPos %worldPos

%view_dir = OpExtInst %vec3 %glsl_std %Normalize %to_camera

%normal = OpExtInst %vec3 %glsl_std %Normalize %normal_raw

%light_dir_norm = OpExtInst %vec3 %glsl_std %Normalize %light_direction

%dot_n_v = OpDot %float %normal %view_dir
%dot_n_v_clamped = OpExtInst %float %glsl_std %FMax %dot_n_v %float_0

%one_minus_dot = OpFSub %float %float_1 %dot_n_v_clamped
%rim = OpExtInst %float %glsl_std %Pow %one_minus_dot %float_3

%dot_n_l = OpDot %float %normal %light_dir_norm
%dot_n_l_clamped = OpExtInst %float %glsl_std %FMax %dot_n_l %float_0

%diffuse = OpFMul %float %dot_n_l_clamped %float_0p5
%diffuse_contrib = OpFMul %float %float_0p5 %diffuse
%modulation_factor = OpFAdd %float %float_0p3 %diffuse_contrib

%base_modulated = OpVectorTimesScalar %vec3 %base_color %modulation_factor

%rim_contribution = OpVectorTimesScalar %vec3 %rim_tint_color %rim

%final_rgb = OpFAdd %vec3 %base_modulated %rim_contribution

%final_color = OpCompositeConstruct %vec4 %final_rgb %float_1

OpStore %out_color %final_color

OpReturn
OpFunctionEnd

SPIR-V Shader - Toon Shading

View SPIR-V (3856 chars)
; SPIR-V
; Version 1.0
; Generator: Manual
; Bound: 300
; Schema: 0

OpCapability Shader

OpMemoryModel Logical GLSL450

OpExtInstImport "GLSL.std.450"

OpEntryPoint Fragment %main "main" %in_worldPos %in_normal %in_uv %in_tangent %in_color %out_color

OpExecutionMode %main OriginUpperLeft

; Input/Output decorations
OpDecorate %in_worldPos Location 0
OpDecorate %in_normal Location 1
OpDecorate %in_uv Location 2
OpDecorate %in_tangent Location 3
OpDecorate %in_color Location 4
OpDecorate %out_color Location 0

; UBO decorations
OpDecorate %ubo_type Block
OpDecorate %ubo_var DescriptorSet 0
OpDecorate %ubo_var Binding 0

; UBO member offsets
OpMemberDecorate %ubo_type 0 Offset 0
OpMemberDecorate %ubo_type 1 Offset 64
OpMemberDecorate %ubo_type 2 Offset 128
OpMemberDecorate %ubo_type 3 Offset 192
OpMemberDecorate %ubo_type 4 Offset 208
OpMemberDecorate %ubo_type 5 Offset 224

; Type declarations
%void = OpTypeVoid
%bool = OpTypeBool
%int = OpTypeInt 32 1
%float = OpTypeFloat 32
%vec2 = OpTypeVector %float 2
%vec3 = OpTypeVector %float 3
%vec4 = OpTypeVector %float 4
%mat4 = OpTypeMatrix %vec4 4

; UBO structure
%ubo_type = OpTypeStruct %mat4 %mat4 %mat4 %vec4 %vec4 %vec4

; Pointer types
%ptr_Uniform_ubo_type = OpTypePointer Uniform %ubo_type
%ptr_Uniform_vec4 = OpTypePointer Uniform %vec4
%ptr_Input_vec3 = OpTypePointer Input %vec3
%ptr_Input_vec2 = OpTypePointer Input %vec2
%ptr_Output_vec4 = OpTypePointer Output %vec4

; Global variables
%ubo_var = OpVariable %ptr_Uniform_ubo_type Uniform
%in_worldPos = OpVariable %ptr_Input_vec3 Input
%in_normal = OpVariable %ptr_Input_vec3 Input
%in_uv = OpVariable %ptr_Input_vec2 Input
%in_tangent = OpVariable %ptr_Input_vec3 Input
%in_color = OpVariable %ptr_Input_vec3 Input
%out_color = OpVariable %ptr_Output_vec4 Output

; Constants
%c_float_0 = OpConstant %float 0.0
%c_float_1 = OpConstant %float 1.0
%c_float_0p25 = OpConstant %float 0.25
%c_float_0p5 = OpConstant %float 0.5
%c_float_0p75 = OpConstant %float 0.75
%c_float_0p2 = OpConstant %float 0.2
%c_float_0p4 = OpConstant %float 0.4
%c_float_0p7 = OpConstant %float 0.7
%c_int_3 = OpConstant %int 3

; Function type
%func_void_type = OpTypeFunction %void

; Main function
%main = OpFunction %void None %func_void_type
%label_main = OpLabel

; Load inputs
%v_worldPos = OpLoad %vec3 %in_worldPos
%v_normal = OpLoad %vec3 %in_normal
%v_color = OpLoad %vec3 %in_color

; Normalize normal vector
%v_normal_norm = OpExtInst %vec3 %1 Normalize %v_normal

; Load lightPos from UBO (member 3, offset 192)
%ptr_lightPos = OpAccessChain %ptr_Uniform_vec4 %ubo_var %c_int_3
%v_lightPos_v4 = OpLoad %vec4 %ptr_lightPos
%v_lightPos = OpVectorShuffle %vec3 %v_lightPos_v4 %v_lightPos_v4 0 1 2

; Calculate light direction: normalize(lightPos - worldPos)
%v_toLight = OpFSub %vec3 %v_lightPos %v_worldPos
%v_lightDir = OpExtInst %vec3 %1 Normalize %v_toLight

; Calculate lambertian diffuse term: max(dot(N, L), 0)
%v_dotNL = OpDot %float %v_normal_norm %v_lightDir
%v_diffuse = OpExtInst %float %1 FMax %v_dotNL %c_float_0

; Quantize into bands using nested select statements
; if diffuse >= 0.75: band = 1.0
; else if diffuse >= 0.5: band = 0.7
; else if diffuse >= 0.25: band = 0.4
; else: band = 0.2

%b_cmp1 = OpFOrdGreaterThanEqual %bool %v_diffuse %c_float_0p75
%v_sel1 = OpSelect %float %b_cmp1 %c_float_1 %c_float_0p7

%b_cmp2 = OpFOrdGreaterThanEqual %bool %v_diffuse %c_float_0p5
%v_sel2 = OpSelect %float %b_cmp2 %v_sel1 %c_float_0p4

%b_cmp3 = OpFOrdGreaterThanEqual %bool %v_diffuse %c_float_0p25
%v_band = OpSelect %float %b_cmp3 %v_sel2 %c_float_0p2

; Multiply vertex color by band value
%v_finalColor = OpVectorTimesScalar %vec3 %v_color %v_band

; Construct output color with alpha = 1.0
%v_output = OpCompositeConstruct %vec4 %v_finalColor %c_float_1

; Store output
OpStore %out_color %v_output

OpReturn

OpFunctionEnd

%void = OpTypeVoid

%vec2 = OpTypeVector %float 2
%vec3 = OpTypeVector %float 3
%vec4 = OpTypeVector %float 4
%mat4 = OpTypeMatrix %vec4 4

%ptr_input_vec3 = OpTypePointer Input %vec3
%ptr_input_vec2 = OpTypePointer Input %vec2

%ptr_uniform_UBO = OpTypePointer Uniform %UBO

%const_0p5 = OpConstant %float 0.5
%const_0p2 = OpConstant %float 0.2
%const_0p9 = OpConstant %float 0.9
%const_1 = OpConstant %float 1.0
%const_8 = OpConstant %float 8.0
%const_int_0 = OpConstant %int 0
%const_int_1 = OpConstant %int 1
%const_int_2 = OpConstant %int 2
%const_int_3 = OpConstant %int 3

%ubo_instance = OpVariable %ptr_uniform_UBO Uniform
%in_worldPos = OpVariable %ptr_input_vec3 Input
%in_normal = OpVariable %ptr_input_vec3 Input
%in_uv = OpVariable %ptr_input_vec2 Input
%in_color = OpVariable %ptr_input_vec3 Input
%out_color = OpVariable %ptr_output_vec4 Output

%main = OpFunction %void None %func_void
%entry = OpLabel

%worldPos = OpLoad %vec3 %in_worldPos
%normal = OpLoad %vec3 %in_normal
%uv = OpLoad %vec2 %in_uv

%normal_norm = OpNormalize %vec3 %normal

%lightPos_ptr = OpAccessChain %ptr_uniform_vec4 %ubo_instance %const_int_3
%lightPos = OpLoad %vec4 %lightPos_ptr
%lightPos_xyz = OpVectorShuffle %vec3 %lightPos %lightPos 0 1 2

%lightDir = OpFSub %vec3 %lightPos_xyz %worldPos
%lightDir_norm = OpNormalize %vec3 %lightDir

%diffuse_raw = OpDot %float %normal_norm %lightDir_norm
%diffuse = OpExtInst %float %glsl FMax %diffuse_raw %const_0

%shading_mul = OpFMul %float %const_0p5 %diffuse
%shading_factor = OpFAdd %float %const_0p5 %shading_mul

%uv_scaled = OpVectorTimesScalar %vec2 %uv %const_8

%uv_x = OpCompositeExtract %float %uv_scaled 0
%uv_y = OpCompositeExtract %float %uv_scaled 1

%uv_x_int = OpConvertFToS %int %uv_x
%uv_y_int = OpConvertFToS %int %uv_y

%sum = OpIAdd %int %uv_x_int %uv_y_int
%parity = OpSMod %int %sum %const_int_2

%is_even = OpIEqual %bool %parity %const_int_0
%checker_color = OpSelect %float %is_even %const_0p9 %const_0p2

%final_intensity = OpFMul %float %checker_color %shading_factor

%out_r = %final_intensity
%out_g = %final_intensity
%out_b = %final_intensity
%out_a = %const_1

%result_color = OpCompositeConstruct %vec4 %out_r %out_g %out_b %out_a

OpStore %out_color %result_color

OpReturn
OpFunctionEnd

%g_shaded = OpFMul %float %g %shading_factor
%b_shaded = OpFMul %float %b %shading_factor
%shaded_vec3 = OpCompositeConstruct %vec3 %r_shaded %g_shaded %b_shaded
%result = OpCompositeConstruct %vec4 %shaded_vec3 %float_1

%float_neg1 = OpConstant %float -1.0
%main = OpFunction %void None %main_func_type
%entry = OpLabel
%uv_val = OpLoad %vec2 %in_uv
%normal_val = OpLoad %vec3 %in_normal
%worldPos_val = OpLoad %vec3 %in_worldPos
%uv_x = OpCompositeExtract %float %uv_val 0
%uv_y = OpCompositeExtract %float %uv_val 1
%cell_x = OpFMul %float %uv_x %float_6_0
%cell_y = OpFMul %float %uv_y %float_6_0
%cell_x_floor = OpExtInst %float %1 Floor %cell_x
%cell_y_floor = OpExtInst %float %1 Floor %cell_y
%cell_x_frac = OpFSub %float %cell_x %cell_x_floor
%cell_y_frac = OpFSub %float %cell_y %cell_y_floor
%dx = OpFSub %float %cell_x_frac %float_0_5
%dy = OpFSub %float %cell_y_frac %float_0_5
%dx_sq = OpFMul %float %dx %dx
%dy_sq = OpFMul %float %dy %dy
%dist_sq = OpFAdd %float %dx_sq %dy_sq
%radius_sq = OpFMul %float %float_0_3 %float_0_3
%in_dot = OpFOrdLessThanEqual %bool %dist_sq %radius_sq
%light_ptr = OpAccessChain %ptr_uniform_vec4 %ubo_var %float_3_0
%light_pos = OpLoad %vec4 %light_ptr
%light_pos_x = OpCompositeExtract %float %light_pos 0
%light_pos_y = OpCompositeExtract %float %light_pos 1
%light_pos_z = OpCompositeExtract %float %light_pos 2
%light_pos_vec3 = OpCompositeConstruct %vec3 %light_pos_x %light_pos_y %light_pos_z
%light_dir = OpFSub %vec3 %light_pos_vec3 %worldPos_val
%light_dir_norm = OpExtInst %vec3 %1 Normalize %light_dir
%normal_norm = OpExtInst %vec3 %1 Normalize %normal_val
%dot_product = OpDot %float %normal_norm %light_dir_norm
%dot_clamped = OpExtInst %float %1 FMax %dot_product %float_0
%shading_factor = OpFMul %float %float_0_7 %dot_clamped
%shading_factor = OpFAdd %float %float_0_3 %shading_factor
%dot_color_r = OpFMul %float %float_1_0 %shading_factor
%dot_color_g = OpFMul %float %float_0_8 %shading_factor
%dot_color_b = OpFMul %float %float_0 %shading_factor
%dot_color_shaded = OpCompositeConstruct %vec3 %dot_color_r %dot_color_g %dot_color_b
%bg_color_r = OpFMul %float %float_0_1 %shading_factor
%bg_color_g = OpFMul %float %float_0_1 %shading_factor
%bg_color_b = OpFMul %float %float_0_3 %shading_factor
%bg_color_shaded = OpCompositeConstruct %vec3 %bg_color_r %bg_color_g %bg_color_b
%final_color = OpSelect %vec3 %in_dot %dot_color_shaded %bg_color_shaded
%final_color_r = OpCompositeExtract %float %final_color 0
%final_color_g = OpCompositeExtract %float %final_color 1
%final_color_b = OpCompositeExtract %float %final_color 2
%final_color_rgba = OpCompositeConstruct %vec4 %final_color_r %final_color_g %final_color_b %float_1_0
OpStore %out_color %final_color_rgba
OpReturn
OpFunctionEnd

%vec4 = OpTypeVector %float 4

%ptr_Uniform_vec4 = OpTypePointer Uniform %vec4

%ptr_Output_vec4 = OpTypePointer Output %vec4

%in_normal = OpVariable %ptr_Input_vec3 Input
%out_color = OpVariable %ptr_Output_vec4 Output

%glsl = OpExtInstImport "GLSL.std.450"

%c_0_0 = OpConstant %float 0.0
%c_0_3 = OpConstant %float 0.3
%c_0_4 = OpConstant %float 0.4
%c_0_5 = OpConstant %float 0.5
%c_0_6 = OpConstant %float 0.6
%c_0_05 = OpConstant %float 0.05
%c_0_15 = OpConstant %float 0.15
%c_1_0 = OpConstant %float 1.0
%int_3 = OpConstant %int 3

%main = OpFunction %void None %void_fn
%entry = OpLabel

%normal_raw = OpLoad %vec3 %in_normal
%normal = OpExtInst %vec3 %glsl Normalize %normal_raw

%light_ptr = OpAccessChain %ptr_Uniform_vec4 %ubo %int_3
%light_vec4 = OpLoad %vec4 %light_ptr
%light_pos = OpVectorShuffle %vec3 %light_vec4 %light_vec4 0 1 2

%light_dir = OpExtInst %vec3 %glsl Normalize %light_pos

%dot_nl = OpDot %float %normal %light_dir
%dot_clamped = OpExtInst %float %glsl FMax %dot_nl %c_0_0
%diffuse_factor = OpFMul %float %dot_clamped %c_0_3

%white = OpCompositeConstruct %vec3 %c_1_0 %c_1_0 %c_1_0
%diffuse_color = OpVectorTimesScalar %vec3 %white %diffuse_factor

%n_y = OpCompositeExtract %float %normal 1
%scaled_ny = OpFMul %float %n_y %c_0_5
%blend_factor = OpFAdd %float %scaled_ny %c_0_5
%blend_clamped = OpExtInst %float %glsl FClamp %blend_factor %c_0_0 %c_1_0

%blend_inv = OpFSub %float %c_1_0 %blend_clamped

%sky_color = OpCompositeConstruct %vec3 %c_0_4 %c_0_6 %c_1_0
%ground_color = OpCompositeConstruct %vec3 %c_0_3 %c_0_15 %c_0_05

%sky_contrib = OpVectorTimesScalar %vec3 %sky_color %blend_clamped
%ground_contrib = OpVectorTimesScalar %vec3 %ground_color %blend_inv

%ambient = OpFAdd %vec3 %sky_contrib %ground_contrib

%final_rgb = OpFAdd %vec3 %ambient %diffuse_color

%result = OpCompositeConstruct %vec4 %final_rgb %c_1_0

OpStore %out_color %result
OpReturn

OpFunctionEnd

%result_vec4 = OpCompositeConstruct %vec4 %result_rgb %float_1_0

OpStore %outColor %result_vec4

OpReturn

%out_color = OpVariable %ptr_output_vec4 Output

%c1 = OpConstant %int 1

%c3 = OpConstant %int 3
%c4 = OpConstant %int 4

%f0p2 = OpConstant %float 0.2
%f0p3 = OpConstant %float 0.3
%f0p7 = OpConstant %float 0.7
%f0p8 = OpConstant %float 0.8
%f1 = OpConstant %float 1.0
%f1p5 = OpConstant %float 1.5
%f3 = OpConstant %float 3.0

%func_type = OpTypeFunction %void
%main = OpFunction %void None %func_type
%entry = OpLabel

%worldPos = OpLoad %vec3 %in_worldPos
%normal = OpLoad %vec3 %in_normal

%camera_ptr = OpAccessChain %ptr_uniform_vec4 %ubo_var %c4

%cameraPos = OpVectorShuffle %vec3 %cameraPos_v4 %cameraPos_v4 0 1 2
%lightPos = OpVectorShuffle %vec3 %lightPos_v4 %lightPos_v4 0 1 2

%to_camera = OpFSub %vec3 %worldPos %cameraPos
%dist_sq = OpDot %float %to_camera %to_camera
%dist = OpExtInst %float %ext_glsl Sqrt %dist_sq

%light_dir_raw = OpFSub %vec3 %lightPos %worldPos
%light_dir = OpExtInst %vec3 %ext_glsl Normalize %light_dir_raw
%normal_norm = OpExtInst %vec3 %ext_glsl Normalize %normal

%diffuse_raw = OpDot %float %normal_norm %light_dir
%diffuse = OpExtInst %float %ext_glsl FMax %diffuse_raw %f0

%scaled_diff = OpFMul %float %f0p7 %diffuse
%lambertian = OpFAdd %float %f0p3 %scaled_diff

%fog_dist_offset = OpFSub %float %dist %f1p5
%fog_raw = OpFDiv %float %fog_dist_offset %f3
%fog_factor = OpExtInst %float %ext_glsl FClamp %fog_raw %f0 %f1

%base_color = OpCompositeConstruct %vec3 %f0p8 %f0p2 %f0p2
%lit_color = OpVectorTimesScalar %vec3 %base_color %lambertian

%fog_color = OpCompositeConstruct %vec3 %f0p7 %f0p7 %f0p8

%one_minus_fog = OpFSub %float %f1 %fog_factor
%lit_contrib = OpVectorTimesScalar %vec3 %lit_color %one_minus_fog
%fog_contrib = OpVectorTimesScalar %vec3 %fog_color %fog_factor
%mixed_color = OpFAdd %vec3 %lit_contrib %fog_contrib

%output = OpCompositeConstruct %vec4 %mixed_color %f1
OpStore %out_color %output

OpReturn
OpFunctionEnd

%float_04 = OpConstant %float 0.4

%warm = OpCompositeConstruct %v3float %float_07 %float_07 %float_04

%result_y = OpCompositeExtract %float %result 1

SPIR-V Shader - Mandelbrot Set

View SPIR-V (4295 chars)
; SPIR-V
; Version: 1.0
; Generator: Manual
; Bound: 200
; Schema: 0
OpCapability Shader
OpMemoryModel Logical GLSL450
OpEntryPoint Fragment %main "main" %in_uv %out_color
OpExecutionMode %main OriginUpperLeft

; Decorations for UBO
OpDecorate %ubo_struct Block
OpMemberDecorate %ubo_struct 0 ColMajor
OpMemberDecorate %ubo_struct 0 MatrixStride 16
OpMemberDecorate %ubo_struct 1 ColMajor
OpMemberDecorate %ubo_struct 1 MatrixStride 16
OpMemberDecorate %ubo_struct 2 ColMajor
OpMemberDecorate %ubo_struct 2 MatrixStride 16
OpMemberDecorate %ubo_struct 3 Offset 192
OpMemberDecorate %ubo_struct 4 Offset 208
OpMemberDecorate %ubo_struct 5 Offset 224
OpDecorate %ubo_buffer DescriptorSet 0
OpDecorate %ubo_buffer Binding 0

; Input/Output decorations
OpDecorate %in_uv Location 2
OpDecorate %out_color Location 0

; Type definitions
%void = OpTypeVoid
%bool = OpTypeBool
%float = OpTypeFloat 32
%int = OpTypeInt 32 1
%vec2 = OpTypeVector %float 2
%vec3 = OpTypeVector %float 3
%vec4 = OpTypeVector %float 4
%mat4 = OpTypeMatrix %vec4 4

; UBO struct and pointer
%ubo_struct = OpTypeStruct %mat4 %mat4 %mat4 %vec4 %vec4 %vec4
%ubo_ptr_type = OpTypePointer Uniform %ubo_struct
%ubo_buffer = OpVariable %ubo_ptr_type Uniform

; Input/Output pointers and variables
%in_ptr_vec2 = OpTypePointer Input %vec2
%out_ptr_vec4 = OpTypePointer Output %vec4
%in_uv = OpVariable %in_ptr_vec2 Input
%out_color = OpVariable %out_ptr_vec4 Output

; Function types and constants
%main_func_type = OpTypeFunction %void
%const_float_0 = OpConstant %float 0.0
%const_float_1 = OpConstant %float 1.0
%const_float_2 = OpConstant %float 2.0
%const_float_3 = OpConstant %float 3.0
%const_float_4 = OpConstant %float 4.0
%const_float_20 = OpConstant %float 20.0
%const_float_0_5 = OpConstant %float 0.5
%const_int_0 = OpConstant %int 0
%const_int_1 = OpConstant %int 1
%const_int_20 = OpConstant %int 20

; Main function
%main = OpFunction %void None %main_func_type
%entry = OpLabel

; Load UV coordinates
%uv = OpLoad %vec2 %in_uv
%uv_x = OpCompositeExtract %float %uv 0
%uv_y = OpCompositeExtract %float %uv 1

; Map to Mandelbrot plane: c = (uv.x*3-2, uv.y*2-1)
%c_real_1 = OpFMul %float %uv_x %const_float_3
%c_real = OpFSub %float %c_real_1 %const_float_2

%c_imag_1 = OpFMul %float %uv_y %const_float_2
%c_imag = OpFSub %float %c_imag_1 %const_float_1

; Initialize z = 0, iteration = 0
%z_real_init = OpConstant %float 0.0
%z_imag_init = OpConstant %float 0.0
%iter_init = OpConstant %int 0

; Loop setup
OpBranch %loop_header

%loop_header = OpLabel
%z_real = OpPhi %float %z_real_init %entry %z_real_new %loop_body
%z_imag = OpPhi %float %z_imag_init %entry %z_imag_new %loop_body
%iter = OpPhi %int %iter_init %entry %iter_inc %loop_body

%iter_cmp = OpSLessThan %bool %iter %const_int_20
OpLoopMerge %loop_exit %loop_body None
OpBranchConditional %iter_cmp %loop_body %loop_exit

%loop_body = OpLabel

; Check escape condition: |z|^2 > 4
%z_real_sq = OpFMul %float %z_real %z_real
%z_imag_sq = OpFMul %float %z_imag %z_imag
%mag_sq = OpFAdd %float %z_real_sq %z_imag_sq
%escape = OpFOrdGreaterThan %bool %mag_sq %const_float_4

OpSelectionMerge %escape_check None
OpBranchConditional %escape %loop_exit %escape_check

%escape_check = OpLabel

; Mandelbrot iteration: z = z^2 + c
; z_real_new = z_real^2 - z_imag^2 + c_real
%z_real_sq_2 = OpFMul %float %z_real %z_real
%z_imag_sq_2 = OpFMul %float %z_imag %z_imag
%z_real_part = OpFSub %float %z_real_sq_2 %z_imag_sq_2
%z_real_new = OpFAdd %float %z_real_part %c_real

; z_imag_new = 2*z_real*z_imag + c_imag
%z_cross = OpFMul %float %z_real %z_imag
%z_cross_2 = OpFMul %float %z_cross %const_float_2
%z_imag_new = OpFAdd %float %z_cross_2 %c_imag

; Increment iteration counter
%iter_inc = OpIAdd %int %iter %const_int_1

OpBranch %loop_header

%loop_exit = OpLabel

; Convert iteration count to normalized value for coloring
%iter_float = OpConvertSToF %float %iter
%t = OpFDiv %float %iter_float %const_float_20

; Color calculation: (t, t*0.5, 1.0-t, 1.0)
%color_r = %t
%color_g = OpFMul %float %t %const_float_0_5
%color_b = OpFSub %float %const_float_1 %t
%color_a = OpConstant %float 1.0

; Construct output color
%out_vec = OpCompositeConstruct %vec4 %color_r %color_g %color_b %color_a

; Write output
OpStore %out_color %out_vec

OpReturn
OpFunctionEnd
