Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes
Node.js: v24.14.0
TypeScript compiler: Version 6.0.3

The grader will run the same program on increasingly large scenarios. Use algorithms
that scale; avoid browser APIs, network access, and external npm dependencies.
Return the complete runnable TypeScript source code.

Choose firebreak cells on a forest map to protect towns from spreading fire.		 0.0000
[32x22 forest, 3 towns] town value burned 27/27, burned cells 663/663, valid cuts 0 (run 0.85s)

Wildfire Firebreak Planner - 32x22 forest, 3 towns

View TypeScript code (3102 chars)
declare const require: any;
const fs = require('fs');
const input: string = fs.readFileSync(0, 'utf8');
const data = JSON.parse(input);

interface Town {
  id: string;
  x: number;
  y: number;
  value: number;
}

interface InputData {
  width: number;
  height: number;
  grid: string[];
  ignitions: [number, number][];
  towns: Town[];
  cutBudget: number;
}

function simulateFireSpread(
  grid: string[],
  ignitions: [number, number][],
  firebreaks: Set<string>,
  width: number,
  height: number
): Set<string> {
  const visited = new Set<string>();
  const queue: [number, number][] = [];

  for (const [x, y] of ignitions) {
    const key = `${x},${y}`;
    if (!firebreaks.has(key)) {
      queue.push([x, y]);
      visited.add(key);
    }
  }

  while (queue.length > 0) {
    const [x, y] = queue.shift()!;

    for (const [dx, dy] of [[0, 1], [0, -1], [1, 0], [-1, 0]]) {
      const nx = x + dx;
      const ny = y + dy;
      const key = `${nx},${ny}`;

      if (nx >= 0 && nx < width && ny >= 0 && ny < height && !visited.has(key) && !firebreaks.has(key)) {
        if (grid[ny][nx] === '.') {
          visited.add(key);
          queue.push([nx, ny]);
        }
      }
    }
  }

  return visited;
}

function solve(data: InputData): [number, number][] {
  const { width, height, grid, ignitions, towns, cutBudget } = data;

  const firebreaks = new Set<string>();

  for (let iteration = 0; iteration < cutBudget; iteration++) {
    const burned = simulateFireSpread(grid, ignitions, firebreaks, width, height);

    let currentSaved = 0;
    for (const town of towns) {
      if (!burned.has(`${town.x},${town.y}`)) {
        currentSaved += town.value;
      }
    }

    let bestCell: string | null = null;
    let bestImprovement = 0;

    const candidates = new Set<string>();
    for (const key of burned) {
      const [x, y] = key.split(',').map(Number);
      for (const [dx, dy] of [[0, 1], [0, -1], [1, 0], [-1, 0]]) {
        const nx = x + dx;
        const ny = y + dy;
        const cKey = `${nx},${ny}`;

        if (nx >= 0 && nx < width && ny >= 0 && ny < height &&
            grid[ny][nx] === '.' && !firebreaks.has(cKey) && !burned.has(cKey)) {
          candidates.add(cKey);
        }
      }
    }

    for (const cKey of candidates) {
      const testFirebreaks = new Set(firebreaks);
      testFirebreaks.add(cKey);
      const testBurned = simulateFireSpread(grid, ignitions, testFirebreaks, width, height);

      let testSaved = 0;
      for (const town of towns) {
        if (!testBurned.has(`${town.x},${town.y}`)) {
          testSaved += town.value;
        }
      }

      const improvement = testSaved - currentSaved;
      if (improvement > bestImprovement) {
        bestImprovement = improvement;
        bestCell = cKey;
      }
    }

    if (bestCell) {
      firebreaks.add(bestCell);
    } else {
      break;
    }
  }

  return Array.from(firebreaks).map(key => {
    const [x, y] = key.split(',').map(Number);
    return [x, y];
  });
}

const result = solve(data as InputData);
console.log(JSON.stringify({ firebreaks: result }));

Test 63: Warehouse Robot Job Planner (TypeScript) Score: 1.29 / 6
Typical Prompt:
Write a complete Node.js TypeScript program for this benchmark task.

Assign warehouse pick jobs to robots.

The input JSON contains:
- `grid`: strings where `.` is open floor and `#` is blocked shelving
- `robots`: objects `{id, x, y}`
- `jobs`: objects `{id, x, y, priority}`

Return JSON:
```
{"assignments": [[jobId, jobId, ...], ...]}
```
The array index is the robot id. Every job must appear exactly once. The grader
uses shortest grid walking distance through open cells and compares total travel
to a deterministic greedy reference.

**Input format:**
The program receives one JSON document on stdin. Use this pattern to read it:
```
declare const require: any; const fs = require('fs'); const input: string = fs.readFileSync(0, 'utf8');
const data = JSON.parse(input);
```

**Output format:**
Print one JSON document to stdout. Do not print logs or explanatory text.

**Runtime environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes
Node.js: v24.14.0
TypeScript compiler: Version 6.0.3

The grader will run the same program on increasingly large scenarios. Use algorithms
that scale; avoid browser APIs, network access, and external npm dependencies.
Return the complete runnable TypeScript source code.
 Prompt Changes:

Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought
Assign warehouse pick jobs to robots moving through aisles and blocked shelves.		 0.3434
[28 jobs, 2 robots] travel 399, reference 137, score 0.343 (run 0.11s)

Warehouse Robot Job Planner - 28 jobs, 2 robots

View TypeScript code (2112 chars)
declare const require: any;
const fs = require('fs');
const input: string = fs.readFileSync(0, 'utf8');
const data = JSON.parse(input);

const grid: string[] = data.grid;
const robots = data.robots;
const jobs = data.jobs;

const distCache = new Map<string, number>();

function bfs(startX: number, startY: number, endX: number, endY: number): number {
  const cacheKey = `${startX},${startY},${endX},${endY}`;
  if (distCache.has(cacheKey)) {
    return distCache.get(cacheKey)!;
  }

  if (startX === endX && startY === endY) {
    return 0;
  }

  const height = grid.length;
  const width = grid[0].length;
  const visited: boolean[][] = Array(height).fill(null).map(() => Array(width).fill(false));
  const queue: Array<[number, number, number]> = [[startX, startY, 0]];
  visited[startY][startX] = true;

  while (queue.length > 0) {
    const [x, y, dist] = queue.shift()!;

    for (const [dx, dy] of [[0, 1], [0, -1], [1, 0], [-1, 0]]) {
      const nx = x + dx;
      const ny = y + dy;

      if (nx >= 0 && nx < width && ny >= 0 && ny < height &&
          !visited[ny][nx] && grid[ny][nx] === '.') {
        if (nx === endX && ny === endY) {
          distCache.set(cacheKey, dist + 1);
          return dist + 1;
        }
        visited[ny][nx] = true;
        queue.push([nx, ny, dist + 1]);
      }
    }
  }

  distCache.set(cacheKey, Infinity);
  return Infinity;
}

const maxRobotId = Math.max(...robots.map((r: any) => r.id));
const assignments: number[][] = Array(maxRobotId + 1).fill(null).map(() => []);

// Greedy: for each job in order, assign to nearest robot
const sortedJobs = [...jobs].sort((a: any, b: any) => a.id - b.id);

for (const job of sortedJobs) {
  let bestRobot = null;
  let bestDist = Infinity;

  for (const robot of robots) {
    const dist = bfs(robot.x, robot.y, job.x, job.y);

    if (dist < bestDist || (dist === bestDist && (bestRobot === null || robot.id < bestRobot.id))) {
      bestDist = dist;
      bestRobot = robot;
    }
  }

  if (bestRobot !== null) {
    assignments[bestRobot.id].push(job.id);
  }
}

console.log(JSON.stringify({ assignments }));
